name: Creamy Polenta
description: Creamy polenta makes a perfect, soft bed for stews, ragùs, and sautéed vegetables or seafood. It takes some time and stirring, but it’s easy to make—there’s no prep work—and worth the effort for the satisfying warmth and comfort it provides.
recipeInstructions: Stir and simmer: Combine the cornmeal, water, and salt in a Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed saucepan. Set the pan over medium-high heat and bring to a simmer, stirring to keep the polenta from forming lumps. Reduce the heat to low and cook, stirring often to prevent the polenta from sticking to the bottom of the pot, until the grains are fully cooked, about 45 minutes.
Serve: When the polenta is done, taste and season with more salt if needed. Remove the pot from the heat and stir in the butter and Parmigiano cheese. Serve immediately.

ai.cliffNotes: A cold-water start, a blend of grinds, and frequent stirring make lump-free creamy polenta.
keyTips: Start the cornmeal in COLD water — it prevents the lumps you get from adding to hot liquid.
Use a blend of fine + medium grind for better texture.
Stir frequently over ~45 min so it hydrates into a creamy porridge without sticking.
